title: HardBoundary Addendum v1 — Assumptions + Supply Chain
description: Typed, auditable declaration of the deployment assumptions from THREAT_MODEL.md and the supply-chain hardening status from SBOM.md. Makes doc-only assumptions into verifier-checkable fields that can be bound into receipts and evidence bundles. A STRICT deployment must have all assumptions=true and supply_chain controls satisfied. Addendum is either inlined into HardBoundaryValidationResult or stored separately (referenced by addendum_digest).

assumptions.description: Operational assumptions that must hold for the THREAT_MODEL controls to be valid. Each boolean asserts the assumption is met in this deployment. An assumption set to false in STRICT profile is a verifier FAIL.

egress_firewall_enforced.description: Network egress deny-all is active for agent workloads. Without this, IAM is the only bypass defense (THREAT_MODEL assumption 1).

gateway_signing_key_protected.description: DOTINK_SECRET_KEY is stored in KMS/HSM, not in env var or filesystem. If false, receipts can be forged (THREAT_MODEL assumption 2).

idp_trusted.description: Identity provider used for break-glass approver verification is not compromised (THREAT_MODEL assumption 3).

disk_encryption_enabled.description: Audit log storage volume is encrypted at rest (THREAT_MODEL assumption 4). Required for audit payload confidentiality.

clock_integrity_protected.description: NTP with authentication (or equivalent) is configured. Break-glass expiry depends on clock integrity (THREAT_MODEL assumption 5).

evidence.description: Optional machine-verifiable evidence for each assumption.

kms_key_ref.description: Reference to KMS key ARN or Vault path for the signing key.

idp_config_ref.description: Reference to IdP OIDC/SAML config or trust document.

disk_encryption_ref.description: Reference to storage encryption config or attestation.

ntp_config_ref.description: Reference to NTP with auth config or chrony/timesyncd attestation.

supply_chain.description: Supply chain hardening controls from SBOM.md. Tracks action items as typed booleans so security teams can audit status without reading a doc.

sbom_digest.description: Content digest of the SBOM document (SBOM.md or machine-readable CycloneDX JSON) for this release.

sbom_format.description: Format of the SBOM document referenced by sbom_digest.

base_image_pinned_to_digest.description: Dockerfile FROM uses sha256 digest, not a mutable tag (e.g. python:3.11-slim@sha256:...). SBOM action item 2.

base_image_digest.description: sha256 digest of the base container image. Populated when base_image_pinned_to_digest=true.

requirements_pinned.description: Python packages declared in requirements.txt with exact versions (not ranges). SBOM action item 1.

ci_vuln_scans_enabled.description: pip-audit / cargo audit / govulncheck run in CI on every build. SBOM action item 3.

ci_vuln_scan_ref.description: Reference to the latest CI vulnerability scan report artifact.

release_signed.description: Release artifacts signed with cosign/sigstore. SBOM action item 4.

attestation.description: Build provenance attestation details. attestor=NONE is valid for DEV/ENTERPRISE; STRICT deployments should target COSIGN or SLSA.

attestor.description: COSIGN=cosign-signed OCI image; SLSA=SLSA provenance level 2+; NONE=not yet implemented.

slsa_level.description: SLSA provenance level (1–4). Only meaningful when attestor=SLSA.

artifact_digests.description: Content digests of the signed/attested release artifacts.

attestation_uri.description: URI to the attestation bundle (OCI manifest ref, Rekor log entry, etc.).

allOf.description: egress_firewall_enforced=false is a hard FAIL — the boundary is not real without it.
gateway_signing_key_protected=false means receipts can be forged — hard FAIL.
